questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘A + B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’; ‘A – B’ means ‘A is the brother of B’; ‘A x B’ means ‘A is the wife of B’ and ‘A ÷ B’ means ‘A is the father of B’. Based on the above, if M + Q – R + K – Y, then how is M related to Y?
questions.explanation: K is brother of Y and R is mother of K, So R is Mother of Y Now Q is brother of R, So Q is Mother’s brother of Y At last, M is mother of Q, Then M will be the Mother’s Mother of Y. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997047cb63449.26543952.png)

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘HIT’ is coded as ‘132’ and ‘SBYZ’ is coded as ‘144’. How will ‘YRF’ be coded in that Language?
questions.explanation: Logic: Sum of opposites × no. of letters = code number Opposite of H=S=19 Opposite of I=R=18 Opposite of T=G=7 Now, 19+18+7=44; 44 × 3=132 For SBYZ, 8+25+2+1=36; 36 × 4=144 Similarly, For YRF, 2+9+21=32; 32 × 3=96

Who was the founder of Arya Mahila Samajh?
questions.explanation: Pandita Ramabai founded the Arya Mahila Samaj in 1882 in Pune. Pandita Ramabai was a social reformer, educator, and advocate for women’s rights in India. She established Arya Mahila Samaj to promote women's education and social upliftment, particularly among widows. The organization worked towards eradicating child marriage and improving the status of women in Hindu society. Later, she founded Sharada Sadan in Mumbai and Mukti Mission in Pune to provide shelter and education for destitute women.

questions.question: Who was the viceroy of India during Formation of Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: The Viceroy of India during the establishment of the Indian National Congress [1885] was Lord Dufferin. Lord Dufferin served as the Viceroy of India from 1884 to 1888. During his tenure: The Indian National Congress [INC] was founded in 1885. He initially saw INC as a platform for dialogue but later viewed it as a potential threat. He played a key role in strengthening British control while managing growing nationalist sentiments in India.

questions.question: Which of the following ruler of Delhi Sultanate established a new department called Dewan-i-khairat?
questions.explanation: Diwan-e-Khairat was a department for charity and social welfare, created to help the poor, widows, and orphans. Firoz Shah Tughlaq [1351–1388] introduced several welfare measures, including hospitals [Dar-ul-Shifa], marriage assistance for poor girls, and employment for orphans.

questions.question: Which of the following traveller visited during the reign of Dev Raya I? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा यात्री देव राय प्रथम के शासनकाल के दौरान आया था?
questions.explanation: Nicolo de Conti was an Italian merchant and traveller from Venice who visited the Vijayanagar Empire around 1420, during the reign of Deva Raya I [1406–1422]. His travel accounts provide valuable insights into the social, economic, and political conditions of South India during that period. >निकोलो डे कोंटी वेनिस के एक इटालियन व्यापारी और यात्री थे, जिन्होंने देव राय प्रथम [1406-1422] के शासनकाल के दौरान 1420 के आसपास विजयनगर साम्राज्य का दौरा किया था। उनके यात्रा विवरण उस अवधि के दौरान दक्षिण भारत की सामाजिक, आर्थिक और राजनीतिक स्थितियों के बारे में बहुमूल्य जानकारी प्रदान करते हैं।

questions.question: Which material is used for the plate used in Kuchipudi dance? >कुचिपुड़ी नृत्य में प्रयुक्त प्लेट के लिए किस सामग्री का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Brass Plate [Tarangam Plate] is used in Kuchipudi. Kuchipudi is a classical dance form from Andhra Pradesh that combines graceful movements, expressions, storytelling, and intricate footwork. One of the most unique and iconic features of Kuchipudi is the performance of dance sequences on a brass plate, known as "Tarangam." >कुचिपुड़ी में पीतल की प्लेट [तरंगम प्लेट] का उपयोग किया जाता है। कुचिपुड़ी आंध्र प्रदेश का एक शास्त्रीय नृत्य रूप है जिसमें सुंदर चाल, भाव, कहानी कहने और जटिल पैरों की हरकतें शामिल हैं। कुचिपुड़ी की सबसे अनोखी और प्रतिष्ठित विशेषताओं में से एक पीतल की प्लेट पर नृत्य अनुक्रमों का प्रदर्शन है, जिसे "तरंगम" के रूप में जाना जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following state celebrates the festival of Heikru Hidongba? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा राज्य हेइकरू हिडोंगबा का त्योहार मनाता है?
questions.explanation: Heikru Hidongba is a traditional boat race festival of Manipur, celebrated by the Meitei community. It is held at the Loktak Lake and other water bodies, showcasing colorful boats, skilled rowers, and traditional music. The festival is associated with rituals and prayers for prosperity and good fortune. It reflects the rich cultural heritage and sporting spirit of Manipur. >हेइकरू हिडोंगबा मणिपुर का एक पारंपरिक नाव दौड़ उत्सव है, जिसे मेइती समुदाय द्वारा मनाया जाता है। यह लोकतक झील और अन्य जल निकायों में आयोजित किया जाता है, जिसमें रंग-बिरंगी नावें, कुशल नाविक और पारंपरिक संगीत का प्रदर्शन किया जाता है। यह त्यौहार समृद्धि और सौभाग्य के लिए अनुष्ठानों और प्रार्थनाओं से जुड़ा है। यह मणिपुर की समृद्ध सांस्कृतिक विरासत और खेल भावना को दर्शाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following pass Connects Annamalai Hills to Nilgiri Hills?
questions.explanation: The Palghat Pass [Palakkad Gap] is a natural mountain pass in the Western Ghats that connects the Anamalai Hills [Tamil Nadu] with the Nilgiri Hills [Kerala]. It serves as an important trade and travel route, allowing the movement of people, goods, and even monsoon winds between the two regions. The pass plays a crucial role in climatic and economic connectivity between Tamil Nadu and Kerala. >पालघाट दर्रा [पलक्कड़ गैप] पश्चिमी घाट में एक प्राकृतिक पहाड़ी दर्रा है जो अन्नामलाई पहाड़ियों [तमिलनाडु] को नीलगिरी पहाड़ियों [केरल] से जोड़ता है। यह एक महत्वपूर्ण व्यापार और यात्रा मार्ग के रूप में कार्य करता है, जिससे दोनों क्षेत्रों के बीच लोगों, माल और यहाँ तक कि मानसूनी हवाओं की आवाजाही होती है। यह दर्रा तमिलनाडु और केरल के बीच जलवायु और आर्थिक संपर्क में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of Physiographical division of India? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन भारत के भौगोलिक प्रभाग का हिस्सा नहीं है?
questions.explanation: There are total six physiographic division in India these are Himalayas, northern Plains, peninsula Plateau, the Great Indian desert, coastal plains and group of islands. >भारत में कुल छह भौगोलिक विभाग हैं: हिमालय, उत्तरी मैदान, प्रायद्वीपीय पठार, महान भारतीय रेगिस्तान, तटीय मैदान और द्वीप समूह।

questions.question: The ______portal is a platform provided by government of India for farmers to attain any information related to agriculture >______पोर्टल भारत सरकार द्वारा किसानों को कृषि से संबंधित कोई भी जानकारी प्राप्त करने के लिए प्रदान किया गया एक मंच है
questions.explanation: The farmer portal is the government initiative that provides farmers with information related to crops, weather, market prices, schemes and best practices to improve productivity >किसान पोर्टल सरकार की पहल है जो किसानों को फसलों, मौसम, बाजार मूल्यों, योजनाओं और उत्पादकता में सुधार के लिए सर्वोत्तम प्रथाओं से संबंधित जानकारी प्रदान करती है।

questions.question: Which of the following is not a metalloid? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा उपधातु नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Metalloids have property of both metals and non-metals and are commonly used in semiconductors, glasses and alloys. Some common examples of metalloids include: 1. Silicon [Si] 2. Boron [B] 3. Arsenic [As] 4. Germanium [Ge] 5. Antimony [Sb] 6. Tellurium [Te] 7. Polonium [Po] >उपधातुओं में धातु और अधातु दोनों के गुण होते हैं और इनका उपयोग आमतौर पर अर्धचालकों, कांच और मिश्रधातुओं में किया जाता है। कुछ सामान्य मेटलॉइड्स के उदाहरण हैं: 1. सिलिकॉन [Si] 2. बोरॉन [B] 3. आर्सेनिक [As] 4. जर्मेनियम [Ge] 5. एंटिमनी [Sb] 6. टेल्यूरियम [Te] 7. पोलोनियम [Po]

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest desert in the world? >निम्नलिखित में से विश्व का सबसे बड़ा रेगिस्तान कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the largest Cold desert in the world While Sahara in Africa is the largest hot desert in the world. Since the question is asking the largest desert in the world and not asking the largest cold or hot desert so the answer would be Antarctica >अंटार्कटिका दुनिया का सबसे बड़ा ठंडा रेगिस्तान है जबकि अफ्रीका में सहारा दुनिया का सबसे बड़ा गर्म रेगिस्तान है। चूंकि सवाल दुनिया के सबसे बड़े रेगिस्तान के बारे में पूछा जा रहा है और सबसे बड़े ठंडे या गर्म रेगिस्तान के बारे में नहीं पूछा जा रहा है, इसलिए इसका जवाब अंटार्कटिका होगा।

questions.question: Which of the following has molecules contain only single bond? >निम्नलिखित में से किस अणु में केवल एकल बंध होता है?
questions.explanation: Methane CH4 contains only single bond and it belongs to the alkane Group, while the other belongs to alkenes and alkynes Group which are double and triple bond respectively. >मीथेन CH4 में केवल एकल बंध होता है और यह एल्केन समूह से संबंधित है, जबकि अन्य एल्केन और एल्काइन समूह से संबंधित हैं जो क्रमशः दोहरे और तिहरे बंध वाले होते हैं

questions.question: Which of the following is not a unit of energy? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन ऊर्जा की इकाई नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Newton is the unit of force not energy. It is named after Sir Isaac Newton. Symbol of Newton is N. >न्यूटन बल की इकाई है, ऊर्जा की नहीं। इसका नाम सर आइज़ैक न्यूटन के नाम पर रखा गया है। न्यूटन का प्रतीक N है।

Pacific Ocean > Atlantic Ocean >Indian Ocean > Antarctic Ocean>प्रशांत महासागर > अटलांटिक महासागर > हिंद महासागर > अंटार्कटिक महासागर

questions.question: Which among the following chemical is used for food preservation?
questions.explanation: Sodium benzoate is a common food preservative that prevents the growth of bacteria and fungi. It is widely used in soft drinks, sauces, and packaged foods. It works best in acidic conditions to extend shelf life. >सोडियम बेंजोएट एक आम खाद्य परिरक्षक है जो बैक्टीरिया और कवक के विकास को रोकता है। इसका व्यापक रूप से शीतल पेय, सॉस और पैकेज्ड खाद्य पदार्थों में उपयोग किया जाता है। यह शेल्फ लाइफ बढ़ाने के लिए अम्लीय परिस्थितियों में सबसे अच्छा काम करता है।

questions.question: Pellagra is a deficiency disease that usually occurs due to lack of which of the following nutrients? >पेलाग्रा एक कमी रोग है जो आमतौर पर निम्नलिखित में से किस पोषक तत्व की कमी के कारण होता है?
questions.explanation: Pellagra is caused by a deficiency of Vitamin B3 [Niacin] or its precursor Tryptophan. It is characterized by the "3 Ds"—Dermatitis, Diarrhea, and Dementia—and, if left untreated, can lead to death. This condition is common in populations with a diet lacking in niacin-rich foods like meat, fish, nuts, and whole grains. >पेलाग्रा विटामिन बी3 [नियासिन] या इसके पूर्ववर्ती ट्रिप्टोफैन की कमी के कारण होता है। यह "3 डी" - डर्माटाइटिस, डायरिया और डिमेंशिया - द्वारा विशेषता है और अगर इसका इलाज न किया जाए तो यह मौत का कारण बन सकता है। यह स्थिति उन लोगों में आम है जिनके आहार में नियासिन युक्त खाद्य पदार्थ जैसे मांस, मछली, नट्स और साबुत अनाज की कमी होती है।

questions.question: Which drainage pattern is formed around a central spire or dome-like structure? >कौन सा जल निकासी पैटर्न एक केंद्रीय शिखर या गुंबद जैसी संरचना के आसपास बनता है?
questions.explanation: A Radial Drainage Pattern develops when rivers originate from a central elevated point, such as a dome, volcanic cone, or hill, and flow outward in different directions. This pattern is commonly seen around volcanoes [e.g., Mount Fujiyama, Mount Vesuvius] and uplifted domes [e.g., Amarkantak Plateau in India, from where the Narmada and Son rivers originate]. >रेडियल ड्रेनेज पैटर्न तब विकसित होता है जब नदियाँ किसी केंद्रीय ऊंचे बिंदु, जैसे कि गुंबद, ज्वालामुखी शंकु या पहाड़ी से निकलती हैं और अलग-अलग दिशाओं में बाहर की ओर बहती हैं। यह पैटर्न आमतौर पर ज्वालामुखियों [जैसे, माउंट फ़ूजीयामा, माउंट वेसुवियस] और ऊपर उठे हुए गुंबदों [जैसे, भारत में अमरकंटक पठार, जहाँ से नर्मदा और सोन नदियाँ निकलती हैं।] के आसपास देखा जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following is the principal strategy adopted by National Literacy Mission for the eradication of illiteracy? >निरक्षरता उन्मूलन के लिए राष्ट्रीय साक्षरता मिशन द्वारा अपनाई गई प्रमुख रणनीति निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी है?
options: Alternative and Innovative Education>वैकल्पिक एवं नवीन शिक्षा
questions.explanation: The National Literacy Mission [NLM], launched in 1988, primarily adopted the Total Literacy Campaign [TLC] as its strategy to eradicate illiteracy among adults. This campaign focused on mass mobilization, volunteer-based teaching, and community participation to make literacy accessible to all, especially in rural and disadvantaged areas. >1988 में शुरू किए गए राष्ट्रीय साक्षरता मिशन [एनएलएम] ने वयस्कों में निरक्षरता को मिटाने के लिए मुख्य रूप से संपूर्ण साक्षरता अभियान [टीएलसी] को अपनी रणनीति के रूप में अपनाया। इस अभियान का उद्देश्य सभी के लिए साक्षरता सुलभ बनाना था, खासकर ग्रामीण और वंचित क्षेत्रों में।

questions.question: Animals possessing ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm as germ layers during embryonic development are called? >भ्रूणीय विकास के दौरान रोगाणु परत के रूप में एक्टोडर्म, मेसोडर्म और एंडोडर्म रखने वाले जानवरों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Triploblastic animals develop from three germ layers—ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm. They exhibit bilateral symmetry and include annelids, mollusks, arthropods, and chordates. In contrast, diploblastic animals have only two germ layers. >ट्रिपलोब्लास्टिक जानवर तीन जर्म परतों से विकसित होते हैं- एक्टोडर्म, मेसोडर्म और एंडोडर्म। वे द्विपक्षीय समरूपता प्रदर्शित करते हैं और इनमें एनेलिड, मोलस्क, आर्थ्रोपोड और कॉर्डेट शामिल हैं। इसके विपरीत, डिप्लोब्लास्टिक जानवरों में केवल दो जर्म परतें होती हैं।

questions.question: Which of the following articles mentions about the Appropriation Bills in the state legislature? >निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा अनुच्छेद राज्य विधानमंडल में विनियोग विधेयक के बारे में उल्लेख करता है?
questions.explanation: Article 114 deals with the Appropriation Bills in the Parliament, which provide legal authority to withdraw funds from the Consolidated Fund of India. Similarly, Article 204 covers Appropriation Bills at the State level for withdrawing funds from the Consolidated Fund of the State. >अनुच्छेद 114 संसद में विनियोग विधेयकों से संबंधित है, जो भारत की संचित निधि से धन निकालने के लिए कानूनी अधिकार प्रदान करता है। इसी तरह, अनुच्छेद 204 राज्य स्तर पर राज्य की संचित निधि से धन निकालने के लिए विनियोग विधेयकों को शामिल करता है।

questions.question: With subject is covered under Part 14A of Indian constitution?
questions.explanation: Part XIV-A of the Indian Constitution deals with Tribunals and was added by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.

questions.question: According to National Multidimensional Poverty Index 2023 [A Progress Review], which state has the highest proportion of multidimensionally poor people [Headcount Ratio]? >राष्ट्रीय बहुआयामी गरीबी सूचकांक 2023 [ए प्रोग्रेस रिव्यू] के अनुसार, किस राज्य में बहुआयामी गरीब लोगों का अनुपात [हेडकाउंट अनुपात] सबसे अधिक है?
questions.explanation: According to the National Multidimensional Poverty Index ([MPI] 2023 – A Progress Review, Bihar has the highest proportion of multidimensional poor people with the highest headcount ratio. The report, released by NITI Aayog, measures poverty based on health, education, and living standards. Despite improvements, Bihar remains the most affected state, followed by Jharkhand and Uttar Pradesh. >राष्ट्रीय बहुआयामी गरीबी सूचकांक एमपीआई 2023 - एक प्रगति समीक्षा के अनुसार, बिहार में बहुआयामी रूप से गरीब लोगों का अनुपात सबसे अधिक है, जिसमें सबसे अधिक जनसंख्या अनुपात है। नीति आयोग द्वारा जारी की गई रिपोर्ट स्वास्थ्य, शिक्षा और जीवन स्तर के आधार पर गरीबी को मापती है। सुधारों के बावजूद, बिहार सबसे अधिक प्रभावित राज्य बना हुआ है, उसके बाद झारखंड और उत्तर प्रदेश हैं।

questions.question: Where does the Nobel Prize ceremony take place annually? >नोबेल पुरस्कार समारोह प्रतिवर्ष कहां आयोजित होता है
questions.explanation: The Nobel Prize Ceremony takes place annually on December 10 in Stockholm, Sweden, except for the Nobel Peace Prize, which is awarded in Oslo, Norway. The event is held at the Stockholm Concert Hall, followed by a banquet at the Stockholm City Hall. The prizes, established by Alfred Nobel's will, are awarded in Physics, Chemistry, Medicine, Literature, and Economic Sciences. >नोबेल पुरस्कार समारोह हर साल 10 दिसंबर को स्टॉकहोम, स्वीडन में होता है, सिवाय नोबेल शांति पुरस्कार के, जो ओस्लो, नॉर्वे में दिया जाता है। यह कार्यक्रम स्टॉकहोम कॉन्सर्ट हॉल में आयोजित किया जाता है, जिसके बाद स्टॉकहोम सिटी हॉल में भोज का आयोजन किया जाता है। अल्फ्रेड नोबेल की वसीयत द्वारा स्थापित ये पुरस्कार भौतिकी, रसायन विज्ञान, चिकित्सा, साहित्य और आर्थिक विज्ञान में दिए जाते हैं।

questions.question: A marketplace in which a final good or service is bought and sold is called? >वह बाजार जिसमें अंतिम वस्तु या सेवा खरीदी और बेची जाती है, उसे क्या कहते हैं?
questions.explanation: A product market is where final goods and services are bought and sold. It involves businesses selling to consumers. Examples include retail stores, e-commerce, and supermarkets. >उत्पाद बाज़ार वह जगह है जहाँ अंतिम वस्तुएँ और सेवाएँ खरीदी और बेची जाती हैं। इसमें उपभोक्ताओं को बेचने वाले व्यवसाय शामिल हैं। उदाहरणों में खुदरा स्टोर, ई-कॉमर्स और सुपरमार्केट शामिल हैं।

questions.question: Match the following Rivers with their respective Tributary? Name of the river Tributary A) Ganga 1) Kameng B) Yamuna 2) Punpun C) Brahmaputra 3) Hindon D) Krishna 4) koyna
questions.explanation: The Punpun River is a tributary of the Ganga River. It originates in the Kaimur Hills and joins the Ganga in Bihar. The Hindon River is a tributary of the Yamuna River. It flows through parts of western Uttar Pradesh and joins the Yamuna near the Delhi-UP border. The Kameng River is a tributary of the Brahmaputra River. It flows through the northeastern part of India, especially in Arunachal Pradesh, before joining the Brahmaputra. The Koyna River is a tributary of the Krishna River. It flows through Maharashtra and merges with the Krishna River near the Sangli district.

questions.question: K being odd number greater than 1, K23 – K can be divisible by which of the following numbers?
questions.explanation: K23 – K = K{K22 – 1} We know that, when n is even, an-bn is always divisible by {a+b}{a-b} ∴ K22 – 1 is always divisible by {K+1}{K-1} It is given that K is an odd number, so, both K-1 and K+1 will be even. Hence, all the options are eliminated except D. As, considering any of the first three options, we’ll get K as an even number.

The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment "She might studies." CONCEPT: • Modal verbs [might, may, can, could, should, etc.] are always followed by the base form [first form] of the verb without -s/-es. • In this sentence, the verb "studies" is incorrectly conjugated after the modal verb "might." • The correct base form of the verb should be "study" instead of "studies." Correct Sentence: "She might study hard to pass the competitive examination."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Corruption should be _______ out with the utmost vigour.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Rooted." Explanation: The word "rooted out" means to completely remove or eliminate something harmful or undesirable, which fits the context of eradicating corruption. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Talked – बात करना / चर्चा करना – To speak or discuss something. Example: "They talked about the new policies in the meeting." • Left – छोड़ देना / रहने देना – To abandon or not take action. Example: "He left his job for better opportunities." • Rooted – समाप्त करना /जड़ से हटाना – To completely remove or destroy something harmful. Example: "The government is determined to root out corruption from the system." • Brought – लाया गया / प्रस्तुत किया गया – To bring or introduce something. Example: "New laws were brought into effect last year."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Both of the players didn’t perform well in the match.
options: No one of the players performed
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Neither of the players performed Explanation: The original sentence "Both of the players didn’t perform well in the match." is incorrect because: • "Both" is not used in negative sentences. Instead, we use "neither" to express a negative meaning for two subjects. • "Neither" means "not either," making the negative auxiliary "didn’t" unnecessary. • "No one of the players" [Option A] is incorrect because "none" refers to more than two people, whereas "neither" is used for two. • "Both the players not performed" [Option C] is grammatically incorrect because "not performed" is not the correct verb structure. Corrected Sentence: "Neither of the players performed well in the match.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: You may go for the trip provided if you take permission from your parents.
You may go for the trip provided you take permission from your parents.
You may go for the trip provided taking permission from your parents.
You may go for the trip provided to you take permission from your parents.
questions.explanation: The grammatically correct sentence is: You may go for the trip provided you take permission from your parents. Explanation: "Provided" is a conjunction that must be followed by a subject and verb to express a condition correctly. Option [B] is correct because "provided" correctly introduces the condition, and "you take permission from your parents" is grammatically sound. Why the other options are incorrect: • Option [A] "provided if” is incorrect because "provided" already means "on the condition that," making "if" redundant. • Option [C] is incorrect because "provided taking permission" is not a correct construction; "provided" should be followed by a clause with a subject and verb, not a gerund. • Option [D] is incorrect because "provided to you take permission" is an incorrect phrase. "Provided" should not be followed by "to you." Therefore, correct sentence is: You may go for the trip provided you take permission from your parents.

questions.question: Identify the word that is Incorrectly spelt.
questions.explanation: "Counsillor" is the incorrectly spelled word. Correct spelling: Councillor or Counsellor Explanation: • Councillor -सदस्य / पार्षद– A member of a council. Example: "She was elected as a city councillor last year." • Counsellor - सलाहकार – A person who gives guidance or advice. Example: "She works as a school counsellor, helping students with their problems." Here are the meanings and correct spellings of all options: • Conjecture - अनुमान – A guess or an opinion based on incomplete information. Example: "His theory was based on pure conjecture." • Cumbersome - भारी-भरकम – Large, heavy, or difficult to handle. Example: "The box was too cumbersome to carry alone." • Custody - हिरासत / संरक्षण – The legal right to take care of someone or something. Example: "The mother was granted custody of the child."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. After hours of arguing, my parents finally gave down and let me go to the concert.
questions.explanation: Use "Gave in." Explanation: The phrase "give in" means to finally agree to something after resisting or to surrender, which fits the context of the sentence. The sentence conveys that after a long argument, the parents finally agreed and allowed their child to go to the concert. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Gave away – दान करना / प्रकट कर देना – To donate or reveal a secret. Example: "She gave away all her old clothes to charity." • Gave in – मान जाना / हार मान लेना – To finally agree or surrender. Example: "After hours of negotiation, they gave in to the demands." • Gave out – बांटना / समाप्त हो जाना – To distribute or to stop working. Example: "The teacher gave out the test papers to the students." • Gave down – Incorrect, as "gave down" is not a valid phrase. Corrected Sentence: "After hours of arguing, my parents finally gave in and let me go to the concert."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - Mickey agreed to forego his holiday plans entirely for the new project.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Abandon Explanation: The word "forego" means to give up or sacrifice something voluntarily, which makes "abandon" the correct synonym. The sentence suggests that Mickey decided to give up his holiday for the new project. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Forego – त्यागना / छोड़ देना – To voluntarily give up or go without something. Example: "She decided to forego dessert to maintain her diet." • Postpone – स्थगित करना / टालना – To delay something to a later time. Example: "The meeting was postponed due to bad weather." • Claim – दावा करना / मांग करना – To assert something as one's right. Example: "He claimed the lost wallet as his own." • Demand – मांग करना / जोर देना – To insist on having something. Example: "The workers demanded higher wages." • Abandon – त्यागना / छोड़ देना – To give up something completely. Example: "The explorers had to abandon their journey due to bad weather."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. PROGRESSIVE
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Conservative Explanation: The word "progressive" means favouring change, innovation, or new ideas. The opposite of this would be "conservative," which means preferring traditional values and resisting change. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Progressive – उन्नतिशील / सुधारवादी – Supporting new ideas, change, and advancements. Example: "The company follows a progressive approach to technology adoption." • Conservative – रूढ़िवादी / पारंपरिक – Favoring tradition and opposing rapid change. Example: "His conservative views made him hesitant to adopt new policies." • Cautious – सतर्क / सावधान – Careful to avoid risks or mistakes. Example: "She was cautious while crossing the busy street." • Wavering – डगमगाना / अस्थिर होना – Being indecisive or uncertain. Example: "His wavering decision-making delayed the project." • Harmony – सामंजस्य / मेल – A state of peace and agreement. Example: "They worked in harmony to complete the project."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. She is very passionate _______ painting and spends hours perfecting her art.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: About Explanation: The adjective "passionate" is always followed by "about" when expressing a strong interest or enthusiasm for something. In this sentence, it means that she has a deep love for painting, making "about" the correct choice. Corrected Sentence: "She is very passionate about painting and spends hours perfecting her art."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Back seat driver
options: A person who prefers to stay silent in discussions
A person who gives unwanted advice or instructions
A person who always sits in the back seat of a vehicle
A skilled driver who teaches others
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A person who gives unwanted advice or instructions. "Back seat driver" – "बिन मांगे सलाह देने वाला व्यक्ति" This idiom refers to someone who gives unnecessary or unwanted advice, especially when they are not in control of a situation. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase originates from passengers in cars who constantly give instructions to the driver, despite not actually driving. Over time, it became a metaphor for people who criticize or instruct a driver while sitting in the passenger seat but can also apply to any situation where someone interferes unnecessarily. Example: "John kept telling me how to do my job even though he had no experience—he's such a back seat driver!" Hindi: "जॉन मुझे मेरे काम पर निर्देश देता रहा, जबकि उसे खुद कोई अनुभव नहीं था—वह सच में एक ‘बैक सीट ड्राइवर’ है!"

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: for him to walk every day.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment: "have been a long distance." CONCEPT: • When a plural noun follows a cardinal adjective [like ten, five, twenty, etc.] but refers to a specific measurement [amount, weight, height, distance, period, etc.], it is treated as a singular unit and requires a singular verb. • Here, "Ten kilometres" refers to a specific distance, so it should take the singular verb "has" instead of "have." Corrected Sentence: "Ten kilometres has been a long distance for him to walk every day."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. SALIENT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Prominent. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Salient – महत्वपूर्ण / प्रमुख – Something that is most noticeable or important. Example: "The report highlighted the salient features of the new policy." • Prominent – प्रसिद्ध / उल्लेखनीय – Standing out or being easily noticeable. Example: "She played a prominent role in the success of the project." • Possible – संभावित / संभव – Something that can happen or be done. Example: "Is it possible to complete the task by tomorrow?" • Polite – विनम्र / शिष्ट – Showing good manners and respect towards others. Example: "He was very polite to the guests." • Ordinary – साधारण / सामान्य – Something that is common or not special. Example: "He had an ordinary day at work."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- He gave up the struggle in DESPAIR.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hope. Explanation: The word "despair" means a complete loss of hope or feeling of hopelessness. The antonym should convey the opposite meaning, referring to a feeling of expectation and optimism. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Despair – निराशा / हताशा – A state of losing all hope. Example: "After failing multiple times, he was in complete despair." • Hope – आशा / उम्मीद – A feeling of expectation or belief that something good will happen. Example: "Despite the difficulties, she never lost hope." • Sorrow – दुख / शोक – A feeling of deep distress caused by loss or disappointment. Example: "She was filled with sorrow after hearing the bad news." • Grief – शोक / गहरा दुख – Intense sadness, usually due to a loss. Example: "He was in deep grief after the passing of his pet." • Misery – कष्ट / दुख – A state of extreme suffering or unhappiness. Example: "The villagers lived in misery due to the drought."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The times that people used ________ boat have become history.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "The times that people used canvas boats have become history." It means that in the past, people used boats made of canvas, but they are no longer in use. Explanation of the Other Options: • Canvas – मजबूत कपड़ा / कैनवास – A strong fabric used for making tents, sails, or boats. Example: "The artist painted on a large canvas." • Canvass – प्रचार करना / राय लेना – To seek support or conduct a survey. Example: "The politician canvassed the neighborhood for votes." • Convex – उभरा हुआ / बाहर की ओर मुड़ा – Having a surface that curves outward. Example: "A magnifying glass has a convex lens." • Canva– Canva is a graphic design tool, not a word related to boats. Conclusion: The correct word is "Canvas," as it correctly refers to the material used for making old-fashioned boats.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who eats human flesh
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Cannibal. Here are the explanations for all options: • Cannibal – "नरभक्षी" – A person who eats human flesh. Example: "The stranded sailors feared turning into cannibals due to starvation." • Omnivore – "सर्वाहारी" – An organism that eats both plants and animals. Example: "Humans are omnivores as they consume both meat and vegetables." • Carnivore – "मांसाहारी" – An animal that feeds primarily on meat. Example: "Lions and tigers are carnivores because they hunt for food." • Glutton – "पेटू / बहुत खाने वाला" – A person who eats excessively. Example: "Despite being on a diet, he behaved like a glutton at the buffet."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. She had summed up his immediate task in a nutshell.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: In a few words. "In a nutshell" – "संक्षेप में / संक्षिप्त रूप से" This idiom means to summarize something concisely, in a brief and clear manner. It is used when giving a short explanation or summary of a situation, idea, or task. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase "in a nutshell" comes from ancient times when Roman philosopher Pliny the Elder mentioned that the entire Iliad [a long epic poem] was once written in tiny handwriting that could fit inside a nutshell. Over time, it came to mean explaining something in the shortest possible way. Example: "The teacher explained the entire chapter in a nutshell, making it easier for students to understand." [This means the teacher gave a brief and clear summary of the chapter.] Hindi Translation: "शिक्षक ने पूरे अध्याय को संक्षेप में समझाया, जिससे छात्रों को समझने में आसानी हुई।"

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. An exact copy of handwritten or a picture produced by a machine
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Facsimile. Meanings and Examples of the Given Options: • Manuscript – "हस्तलिखित पांडुलिपि" – A handwritten or typed document, especially a draft of a book or article. Example: "The author submitted his manuscript to the publisher for review." • Copywrite – "कॉपीराइट" – The act of securing legal rights for an original written work. Example: "He applied to copywrite his new novel to prevent plagiarism." • Fake – "नकली / जाली" – Something that is not genuine or authentic. Example: "The store was caught selling fake designer watches." • Facsimile – "प्रतिलिपि / हूबहू नकल" – An exact copy of a document, handwriting, or image produced by a machine. Example: "The museum displayed a facsimile of the ancient manuscript."

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. She quickly grabbed an umbrella and rushed outside. B. Just as she stepped out, she saw a little kitten shivering in the rain. C. Emma was reading a book when she heard the sound of heavy rain. D. She gently picked up the kitten and carried it inside to keep it warm.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: CBAD This order makes sense because: • Sentence C introduces the main topic: Emma hearing the rain while reading. • Sentence B expands on what she discovers outside—a shivering kitten in the rain. • Sentence A describes her immediate reaction—grabbing an umbrella and rushing outside. • Sentence D concludes the paragraph by showing her kindness in rescuing the kitten.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. They will complete the project next week.
options: The project was completed by them next week.
The project will be completed by them next week.
The project is completed by them next week.
The project has been completed by them next week.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The project will be completed by them next week. Explanation: • The given sentence follows the active voice structure: Subject + will + base verb (V1) + object + time expression [They will complete the project next week]. • To convert it into passive voice, we use the structure: Object + will be + past participle (V3) + by + subject + time expression [The project will be completed by them next week].

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Mother said to the baby, “Don’t play with fire.”
options: Mother warned the baby to play with fire.
Mother warned the baby not to play with fire.
Mother forbade the baby not to play with fire.
Mother requested the baby not to play with fire.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mother warned the baby not to play with fire. Explanation: The given sentence is in direct speech and follows the structure: Mother said to the baby, "Don’t play with fire." To convert it into indirect speech, follow these rules: • "Said to" changes to "warned" [since it is a cautionary statement]. • The imperative sentence ["Don’t play with fire"] is converted using "to/not to + base verb [V1]." • "Don’t play" changes to "not to play" in indirect speech.

questions.question: Today’s women are rational in approach, careful in handling situations and want to do things as best as possible. The 4th World Conference of Women held in Beijing in September 1995 had ____1____ that no enduring solution of society’s most threatening social, economic and political problems could be found without the participation and empowerment of women. It had also recognized the ____2____ role of women in eradicating poverty and mending the social fabric. The Constitution of India has ____3____ on women equal rights, opportunities and of employment with men. Education is the main instrument through which we can narrow ____4____ the prevailing inequality and accelerate the process of economic and political change in the status of women. A recent World Bank study says that educating girls is not a charity, it is good economics and if developing nations are to ____5____ poverty, they must educate the girls. The report says that the economic and social returns on investment in education of the girls considerably affect the human development index of the nation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The correct answer is: Emphasized. It fits the context perfectly. "Emphasized" implies that the conference highlighted or stressed the importance of women's participation and empowerment. The sentence suggests that the conference drew attention to the crucial role women play in solving societal problems. Let's see other options. Option A: Entangled- Meaning: To twist or turn something together, often in a complicated or confusing way. This option doesn't fit the context. "Entangled" implies a complicated or confusing situation, which isn't relevant to the conference's declaration. The sentence requires a word that conveys emphasis or importance, not complexity. Option B: Overlooked - Meaning: To fail to notice or consider something. This option doesn't fit the context either. "Overlooked" implies ignoring or neglecting something, which isn't what the conference intended. The conference aimed to highlight women's empowerment, not overlook it. Option D: Ostracized - Meaning: To exclude or banish someone from a group or society. This option doesn't fit the context at all. "Ostracized" implies exclusion or banishment, which is the opposite of what the conference aimed to achieve. The conference sought to include and empower women, not exclude them.
